channel.title: AdvisoryOps — FDA Class III Medical Device Advisories
channel.description: Advisories affecting FDA Class III (highest-risk) medical devices

item.title: Defibrillator, Automatic Implantable Cardioverter, With Cardiac Resynchronization (Crt-D) recall
item.description:
Two corrections to firmware due to Skipped-Charge Shock and Sensor Noise Anomaly are being made with one noninvasive upgrade. | Implantable Cardioverter Defibrilators
Epic DR/HF (V-233/V-337/V-338), Epic Plus DR/VR/HF (V-236/V-239/V-196/V-239T/V-196T/V-350)
Atlas DR (V242), and Atlas Plus DR/VR/HF (V-243/V-193C/V-340/V-341/V-343). | Device: Defibrillator, Automatic Implantable Cardioverter, With Cardiac Resynchronization (Crt-D).

item.title: Automated External Defibrillators (Non-Wearable) recall (Philips Medical Systems)
item.description: In Software versions below A.02.00, with the sync function enabled and the device switched from manual to AED, there may be an unexpected delay in delivery of energy | Philips Heartstart MRx Monitor/Defibrillator Models: M3535A (Hospital Use) , M3536A (EMS Use) | Philips Medical Systems | Device: Automated External Defibrillators (Non-Wearable).

item.title: Implantable Pacemaker Pulse-Generator recall (Cook Biotech, Inc.)
item.description: Adverse events- suspected pseudoaneurysm | Surgisis peripheral vascular patch; catalog number C-VSLH-4S-1x10. | Cook Biotech, Inc. | Device: Implantable Pacemaker Pulse-Generator.

item.title: Automated External Defibrillators (Non-Wearable) recall (Cardiac Science Corporation)
item.description: Some Powerheart AED Model 9300A may contain a suspect electronic component and there is a remote likelihood that the function of this device may be interrupted during the charge. | Powerheart AED G3 Automated External Defibrillator Model 9300A (Automatic). Cardiac Science AEDs are manufactured by: Manufacturing: Cardiac Science, Inc., 5474 Feltl Road, Minnetonka, MN 55343-7982. | Cardiac Science Corporation | Device: Automated External Defibrillators (Non-Wearable).

item.title: Automated External Defibrillators (Non-Wearable) recall (Heartsine Technologies Inc)
item.description: The affected Automatic External Defibrillators (AED) annouce a fault warning and shut themselves off before a shock can be delivered to the patient. This is reportedly due to slow capacitor charging rates which are interpreted by the AED software as a fault condition. | Heartsine Samaritan AED Defibrillators - Models: SAM 001, SAM 002, SAM 003. Serial numbers within range of 1270 - 2324 (not all are affected). | Heartsine Technologies Inc | Device: Automated External Defibrillators (Non-Wearabl

item.title: Automated External Defibrillators (Non-Wearable) recall (Medtronic Emergency Response Systems, Inc.)
item.description: The LIFEPAK 12 biphasic defibrillator/ monitors may deliver inappropriate energy levels and fail to escalate energy levels when configured to do so. | LIFEPAK 12 defibrillator/ monitors biphasic | Medtronic Emergency Response Systems, Inc. | Device: Automated External Defibrillators (Non-Wearable).

item.title: Implantable Cardioverter Defibrillator (Non-Crt) recall (Biotronik Inc)
item.description: Potential for early depletion of Implantable Cardioverter Defibrillator battery. | Belos VR Implantable Cardioverter Defibrillator (ICD) with version A-K00.1.U/1 programmer software. | Biotronik Inc | Device: Implantable Cardioverter Defibrillator (Non-Crt).

item.title: Automated External Defibrillators (Non-Wearable) recall (Philips Medical Systems)
item.description: The device may disarm and not deliver shock when performing a Synchronized Cardioversion | Philips Medical HeartStart MRx Monitor/Defibrillator Model: M3535A, M3536A | Philips Medical Systems | Device: Automated External Defibrillators (Non-Wearable).
